title: My Husband Gets Therapy | #shorts
full_text: So Shalam, today I'm going to give you a gift. I'm going to be your therapist. Forget I'm your wife. I forgot that 10 years ago. Okay, we are not making jokes right now. Shalom. Whatever is on your heart, you say it with no fear. I mean, it's everything has become so expensive. Zoa is already in college. Brij is going to Cornell this fall. Two tuition and the third one coming up. I mean, that's a real source of stress. Yeah, I understand that. You just keep making more money and make more than you make right now and that will fix that. Okay, next. Okay. Um, then I also am very stressed out about health. Whereas my wife is growing older. She may need more support. And even though I don't age as much, I age, too. You do age. I mean, I have seen signs of you aging. Your wife looks pretty youthful. I see her in the news all the time. I feel like you're just creating problems where no problems exist. My aging parents are parents are 5,000 miles away and I really miss them. That sounds like good news right now. They want to see me. They want to see my children. They want to spend time with my with their daughter-in-law who doesn't do any daughter-in-lawy things. So, I'm going to ask you very clearly. I'm all about results. In a fight between your mom and your wife, whose side do you take? My mother.
